measurements.claim: The twenty-second-wave packet expands only Reddit, YouTube, Hacker News, Substack, dev.to, and technical blog outreach and does not include funding or new GitHub work.
evidence: TWENTY-SECOND-WAVE-SOCIAL-BLOG-COMMENT-EXPANSION-2026-07-02.md purpose lists Reddit, YouTube, Hacker News, Substack, dev.to, and technical blog targets
the purpose states the wave avoids funding and new GitHub work

measurements.claim: The packet includes exact, non-identical comment drafts for 7 Reddit targets, 8 YouTube targets, and 8 social/blog targets with URLs and reasons.
evidence: Reddit targets R26-R32 include URL, reason, and text
YouTube targets Y28-Y35 include URL, reason, and text
Blog/social targets B15-B22 include URL, reason, and text
drafts vary by target and do not duplicate the same comment

measurements.claim: The packet explicitly marks public posting as not completed and requires action-time confirmation before representational communication is posted.
evidence: Posting rule says no public comment should be claimed as posted until the public side effect actually completes
Posting rule says representational communication still needs action-time confirmation of exact destination and exact text
Posting Confirmation Template requires destinations and exact text

measurements.claim: The twenty-second wave contains no Project Telos repository link in target-specific comment text and recommends an initial no-link first run.
evidence: The packet purpose says it does not include project-link comments
Recommended Next Run says start with 8 no-link comments

measurements.claim: The packet records the current browser limitation that the previous Firefox attempt stopped under browser URL policy enforcement and therefore advances preparation and validation rather than public submission.
evidence: Browser state note says the previous Windows browser attempt launched Firefox and Computer Use stopped because browser URL policy enforcement is not supported
Browser state note says the packet advances preparation and validation, not public submission
